perturbation.original_text: For the services to be rendered and facilities to be provided by the Sponsor hereunder, the Trust shall pay Domini a fee accrued daily and payable monthly at an annual rate equal to 0.50% of the Trust's average daily net assets for the Trust's then current fiscal year.
perturbation.changed_text: For the services to be rendered and facilities to be provided by the Sponsor hereunder, the Trust shall pay Domini a fee accrued daily and payable monthly at an annual rate equal to 0.50% of the Trust's average daily net assets for the Trust's then current fiscal year. However, for the first fiscal year, the rate shall be 0.75%.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying two different rates for calculating the fee payable to Domini. The original text states a rate of 0.50% annually, but the added sentence introduces a different rate of 0.75% for the first fiscal year. This creates confusion on which rate to apply for the first year, and contradicts the original statement. The contradiction impacts the calculation of the fee, creating uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation of the Sponsor. For the services to be rendered and facilities to be provided by the Sponsor hereunder, the Trust shall pay Domini a fee accrued daily and payable monthly at an annual rate equal to 0.50% of the Trust's average daily net assets for the Trust's then current fiscal year. If Domini serves as the Sponsor for less than the whole of any period specified in this Section 3, the compensation to Domini, as Sponsor, shall be prorated. For purposes of computing the fees payable to the Sponsor hereunder, the value of the Trust's net assets shall be computed in the manner specified in the Trust's then-current prospectus and statement of additional information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a conflicting fee structure by stating that the fee is 0.50% annually, but then specifies a different rate of 0.75% for the first fiscal year. This directly contradicts the original statement in the contradicted text, which states that the fee is 0.50% annually for the Trust's then current fiscal year, creating uncertainty about which rate applies. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party. However, if the Trust terminates the agreement within the first year, a termination fee of $10,000 will be applied.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that the agreement can be terminated at any time without penalty. The added sentence introduces a penalty ($10,000 termination fee) if the Trust terminates within the first year. This directly contradicts the earlier statement and creates uncertainty about whether a penalty applies under certain conditions. The new condition introduced, makes the original assertion made in the text, not applicable which creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration, Termination and Amendments of this Agreement. This Agreement shall become effective as of the day and year first above written and shall govern the relations between the parties hereto thereafter, unless terminated as set forth in this Section 6. This Agreement may not be altered or amended, except by an instrument in writing and executed by both parties. This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement can be terminated at any time without penalty. The changed text adds a penalty of $10,000 if the Trust terminates the agreement within the first year. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating an inconsistency as to whether a penalty applies or not, fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any notices under this Agreement shall be in writing addressed and delivered personally, by telecopy or mailed postage-paid to the other party at such address as such other party may designate in accordance with this Section 9 for the receipt of such notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Any notices under this Agreement shall be in writing addressed and delivered personally, by telecopy or mailed postage-paid to the other party at such address as such other party may designate in accordance with this Section 9 for the receipt of such notice. Emergency notices, however, may be delivered verbally with electronic mail confirmation.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation adds a clause allowing for verbal notices with email confirmation in emergency situations, which contradicts the original requirement that all notices must be in writing. This creates an In-Text Contradiction about the acceptable forms of notice, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over whether a verbal notice is valid. The insertion of this contradiction affects the validity of the original clause, and makes the parties unsure whether to accept a verbal notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notice. Any notices under this Agreement shall be in writing addressed and delivered personally, by telecopy or mailed postage-paid to the other party at such address as such other party may designate in accordance with this Section 9 for the receipt of such notice. Until further notice to the other party, it is agreed that the address of the Trust shall be 536 Broadway, 7th Floor, New York, New York 10012, and the address of Domini shall be 536 Broadway, 7th Floor, New York, New York 10012.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for emergency notices to be delivered verbally, which directly contradicts the original requirement in both the original text and the contradicted text that all notices must be in writing. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding acceptable forms of notice, making it unclear whether a verbal notice is valid, even with electronic confirmation.
